I have reviewed the summary carefully for internal contradictions—specifically checking for the same metric, index, or count attributed to different values, dates, or time periods.

- Qualitative descriptions without numeric conflicts

The summary is internally consistent.

**UNCHANGED:**

AI shopping agents are demonstrating significant and growing commercial deployment, with key players like Google and Perplexity making strides.

Google's Universal Cart, launched at I/O 2026, is rolling out across Search and the Gemini app in the U.S. this summer, with YouTube and Gmail to follow [blog.google](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/shopping/google-shopping-cart/). It allows users to add items from various services to a single cart and check out securely using Google Pay or transfer to merchant sites. Google's Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) will enable secure agentic payments with user-defined guardrails. UCP-powered checkout is also expanding to Canada, Australia, and the U.K. in the coming months, and will include hotel booking and local food delivery soon [blog.google](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/shopping/google-shopping-cart/).

Perplexity's Buy Now shopping agent has reached two million monthly active shoppers and a two-billion-dollar annualized run rate in merchandise volume as of July 2, 2026 [meet.one](https://meet.one/perplexity-shopping-agent-two-million-buyers/). It expanded in-app checkout to all Shopify merchants in January through the Perplexity Merchant Program and made agentic shopping free for U.S. users in February [meet.one](https://meet.one/perplexity-shopping-agent-two-million-buyers/). HSBC UK and Visa also completed one of the first live end-to-end agentic transactions using a HSBC UK card this week, with plans to scale the technology through existing card networks [fintechmagazine.com](https://fintechmagazine.com/news/will-hsbc-visa-ai-agents-change-how-we-shop). While challenges exist, such as Amazon restricting Perplexity's access [meet.one](https://meet.one/perplexity-shopping-agent-two-million-buyers/) and past difficulties in onboarding merchants for in-chat checkout [thedroidsonroids.com](https://www.thedroidsonroids.com/blog/what-is-agentic-commerce/), the rapid user adoption and expansion by major platforms suggest significant commercial deployment is underway.

The New World screwworm has reappeared in the contiguous U.S. in 2026, with 32 animal cases confirmed in Texas and one in New Mexico as of July 5. These are the first livestock cases in the U.S. in about five decades [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/how-did-screwworm-get-to-us). The affected animals include cows, goats, sheep, and one dog, which was the single case in Lea County, New Mexico [apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/screwworm-flesh-eating-parasite-cattle-texas-813099c492b7b9607e087dd3cca58457), [sciencenews.org](https://www.sciencenews.org/article/new-world-screwworm-spread-us-eradicate).

The origin of these infections is still unclear. While initially thought the New Mexico dog might have contracted the parasite from Mexico, the state's Department of Agriculture later stated the animal had not been south of the border, suggesting local transmission [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/how-did-screwworm-get-to-us). There have been no reported cases in wild animals and no wild screwworm flies caught in the U.S. so far, according to the USDA [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/how-did-screwworm-get-to-us). However, a forecasting question specifically asks about wild animal infection before 2027 [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/36478/wild-animal-infected-with-new-world-screwworm-in-the-us-before-2027/).

Quarantine zones are in place in parts of over a dozen Texas counties, and some states have restricted livestock movement from Texas [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/how-did-screwworm-get-to-us). Officials are working on wildlife surveillance methods, including drones and AI [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/how-did-screwworm-get-to-us). Climate change contributing to warmer temperatures is cited as a key factor expanding the fly's habitat northward [apnews.com

The summary states two different seat counts for the current House composition:

These cannot both be current figures. Here is the corrected summary:

As of July 19, 2026, the Republican Party holds a narrow 217-212 majority in the U.S. House of Representatives, with one independent member and five vacant seats. Democrats need a net gain of six seats to take control [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html), which would bring them to 218 seats (the number needed for a majority) [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html). Election forecasters like The Cook Political Report, Sabato's Crystal Ball, and Inside Elections, despite slight differences, generally indicate a tight contest for the upcoming November elections. While these forecasts suggest Republicans are better positioned to retain control, Democrats could win the majority if they sweep the remaining toss-up districts [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html). For example, The Cook Political Report rates 18 toss-up districts [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html), and if Democrats win 13 of those, they would secure 218 seats [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html). Prediction markets, such as Kalshi and Polymarket, currently offer a more favorable outlook for Democrats, giving them a 78% and 82% chance, respectively, of winning control of the House [yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/republicans-chances-losing-control-house-164948385.html).

Note: Discarded the 119th Congress projection (220 Republicans to 215 Democrats) in favor of the more recent July 19, 2026 count (217-212).

I found one contradiction in the summary:

The summary states that "Prior to June 2025, Iran was estimated to be only months away from having enough weapons-grade uranium for a bomb" but also states that "U.S. intelligence assessments in May 2026 suggested that the time Iran would need to build a nuclear weapon had not changed since summer 2025, remaining at about nine months to a year."

These statements are contradictory regarding Iran's timeline to weapons capability. The first suggests Iran was very close (months away) before June 2025, while the second indicates that as of May 2026, the timeline was 9-12 months and hadn't changed since summer 2025. This represents an inconsistency in the assessed timeline.

However, upon closer inspection, both statements could technically be referring to different metrics (one about weapons-grade uranium stockpile, one about time to build a complete weapon), so they may not be strictly contradictory.

After careful review, I find **no definitive internal contradictions** that meet the strict criterion of the same metric being attributed to clearly different values across dates in a way that cannot be reconciled.

**UNCHANGED:**

As of July 19, 2026, reports indicate that Iran's nuclear weapons program has been severely damaged by recent U.S. and Israeli military strikes. U.S. intelligence assessments in May 2026 suggested that the time Iran would need to build a nuclear weapon had not changed since summer 2025, remaining at about nine months to a year, despite two months of war [al-monitor.com](https://www.al-monitor.com/originals/2026/05/exclusive-us-intelligence-indicates-limited-new-damage-irans-nuclear-program). Prior to June 2025, Iran was estimated to be only months away from having enough weapons-grade uranium for a bomb [english.elpais.com](https://english.elpais.com/international/2026-07-05/david-albright-nuclear-expert-the-only-way-to-stop-a-nuclear-weapons-program-is-for-the-country-itself-to-decide-to-do-so.html).

The attacks destroyed or badly damaged Iran's three known enrichment plants [al-monitor.com](https://www.al-monitor.com/originals/2026/05/exclusive-us-intelligence-indicates-limited-new-damage-irans-nuclear-program). The underground enrichment plant at Natanz was likely at least badly damaged, and the above-ground plant was destroyed [al-monitor.com](https://www.al-monitor.com/originals/2026/07/explainer-what-pickaxe-mountain-iranian-nuclear-linked-site-threatened-trump). Experts believe Iran would need a year or more to rebuild its capabilities to build a nuclear weapon [english.elpais.com](https://english.elpais.com/international/2026-07-05/david-albright-nuclear-expert-the-only-way-to-stop-a-nuclear-weapons-program-is-for-the-country-itself-

research_texts.refresh_42082_20260719:
I found no internal contradictions in this summary. All dates, certifications timelines, and programmatic details are internally consistent:

- Joby's Stage 4 status with mid-2027 type certification target is consistent with its late 2026/early 2027 commercial launch target
- Archer (2028+) and Beta Technologies (ALIA CX300 in H2 2027, ALIA 250 in 2028) have distinct timelines with no conflicts
- The eIPP timeline (began July 2026, concluding around 2029) is consistent with the discussion of passenger flight targeting before conclusion
- The FAA's mixed messages about revenue-generating flights under eIPP are presented as a documented inconsistency in the FAA's own positions, not an error in the summary itself

The summary should be returned unchanged.

---

No FAA-certified eVTOL passenger service is expected to be open to the general public in a U.S. city before January 1, 2027.

Analysts and industry reports indicate that no U.S. eVTOL manufacturer is likely to receive full FAA Type Certification in 2026 [lowaltitudeeconomy.aero]. Joby Aviation, currently in Stage 4 of the five-stage certification process, has an earliest realistic path to type certification in mid-2027, with a 20-30% probability, and a commercial launch target of late 2026 or early 2027 for markets like New York and Los Angeles [lowaltitudeeconomy.aero]. Archer Aviation is projected for 2028 or later [lowaltitudeeconomy.aero]. Beta Technologies' ALIA CX300 is targeting FAA type certification in the second half of 2027, and its true eVTOL, the ALIA 250, is targeting certification in 2028 [techtimes.com].

While the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP) began its first operational flights in July 2026 and allows for operations with non-certificated aircraft under specific conditions, there are mixed messages from the FAA regarding revenue-generating passenger flights within this program [theaircurrent.com]. Deputy FAA Administrator Chris Rocheleau stated that such operations would not occur under eIPP with non-certificated aircraft, though the FAA later clarified it is "exploring pathways" [theaircurrent.com]. The eIPP is expected to target passenger flights before its conclusion around 2029 [techtimes.com]. Significant infrastructure, such as dedicated vertiports, is also largely absent [lowaltitudeeconomy.aero].
research_texts.refresh_41299_20260719:
It is highly unlikely that the Insurrection Act or martial law will be declared in relation to the 2026 U.S. midterms. The Trump administration has explicitly stated that concerns about deploying troops to polling places are "baseless conspiracy theories," with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles calling the idea "categorically false" in late 2025 [votebeat.org](https://www.votebeat.org/2026/01/20/trump-national-guard-troops-polling-places-2026-election-insurrection-act/).

Legally, federal law prohibits stationing troops at polling places unless necessary to repel "armed enemies of the United States," and it's illegal for military members to interfere with voting or election officers [votebeat.org](https://www.votebeat.org/2026/01/20/trump-national-guard-troops-polling-places-2026-election-insurrection-act/). While the Insurrection Act allows the president to deploy military forces domestically in emergencies, legal experts from the Brennan Center for Justice state that the Act wouldn't override these specific election laws [votebeat.org](https://www.votebeat.org/2026/01/20/trump-national-guard-troops-polling-places-2026-election-insurrection-act/). The Supreme Court has also intervened to limit Trump's National Guard deployments, requiring gubernatorial consent in some cases. As of December 2025, the Supreme Court issued a temporary restraining order preventing troop deployment to Illinois without the Democratic governor's consent [votebeat.org](https://www.votebeat.org/2026/01/20/trump-national-guard-troops-polling-places-2026-election-insurrection-act/). This suggests significant legal and practical barriers to such actions.
